\chapter{Boolean Analysis â€” One Bit}
%%% generated by the blueprint pipeline from TCSlib.BooleanAnalysis.Hypercontractivity.OneBit
\section{Overview}

This module establishes hypercontractivity for functions on the single-bit cube
$\{\bbf\}^1$. It develops the Fourier description of one-bit functions, the core
real two-point inequality, and derives both the $(p,2)$- and $(2,q)$-hypercontractivity
theorems for one bit (together with the noise-operator duality and the supporting
power-mean, Cauchy--Schwarz, and H\"older-sharpness lemmas).

%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%
\section{Declarations}

\begin{lemma}[Enumeration of the one-bit cube]
\lean{OneBit.boolCube1_univ}
\leanok
\uses{BooleanAnalysis.BoolCube}
The universal finite set of points of $\mathrm{BoolCube}\,1$ equals the two-element set
$\{(\_ \mapsto \mathit{false}),\ (\_ \mapsto \mathit{true})\}$.
\end{lemma}

\begin{lemma}[Enumeration of subsets of $\mathrm{Fin}\,1$]
\lean{OneBit.finsetFin1_univ}
\leanok
The universal finite set of subsets of $\mathrm{Fin}\,1$ equals $\{\emptyset,\ \{0\}\}$.
\end{lemma}

\begin{lemma}[The two one-bit inputs differ]
\lean{OneBit.boolCube1_ne}
\leanok
The constant-$\mathit{false}$ and constant-$\mathit{true}$ inputs on $\mathrm{Fin}\,1$ are
distinct.
\end{lemma}

\begin{lemma}[Empty set differs from $\{0\}$ in $\mathrm{Fin}\,1$]
\lean{OneBit.finsetFin1_ne}
\leanok
The empty subset of $\mathrm{Fin}\,1$ is not equal to $\{0\}$.
\end{lemma}

\begin{lemma}[One-bit value at $\mathit{false}$]
\lean{OneBit.one_bit_val_false}
\leanok
\uses{BooleanAnalysis.BooleanFunc, BooleanAnalysis.boolToSign, BooleanAnalysis.chiS, BooleanAnalysis.fourierCoeff, BooleanAnalysis.walsh_expansion, OneBit.finsetFin1_ne, OneBit.finsetFin1_univ}
For a one-bit function $f$, its value on the all-$\mathit{false}$ input is the sum of its
two Fourier coefficients: $f(\mathit{false}) = \widehat{f}(\emptyset) + \widehat{f}(\{0\})$.
\end{lemma}

\begin{lemma}[One-bit value at $\mathit{true}$]
\lean{OneBit.one_bit_val_true}
\leanok
\uses{BooleanAnalysis.BooleanFunc, BooleanAnalysis.boolToSign, BooleanAnalysis.chiS, BooleanAnalysis.fourierCoeff, BooleanAnalysis.walsh_expansion, OneBit.finsetFin1_ne, OneBit.finsetFin1_univ}
For a one-bit function $f$, its value on the all-$\mathit{true}$ input is the difference of
its two Fourier coefficients:
$f(\mathit{true}) = \widehat{f}(\emptyset) - \widehat{f}(\{0\})$.
\end{lemma}

\begin{lemma}[Expected square of the noised one-bit function]
\lean{OneBit.expect_noiseOp_sq_one_bit}
\leanok
\uses{BooleanAnalysis.BooleanFunc, BooleanAnalysis.expect, BooleanAnalysis.fourierCoeff, BooleanAnalysis.innerProduct, BooleanAnalysis.noiseOp, BooleanAnalysis.noiseOp_fourier, BooleanAnalysis.parseval, OneBit.finsetFin1_ne, OneBit.finsetFin1_univ}
For a one-bit function $f$ and noise rate $\rho$,
\[
  \E\big[(T_\rho f)^2\big] = \widehat{f}(\emptyset)^2 + \rho^2\,\widehat{f}(\{0\})^2 .
\]
\end{lemma}

\begin{lemma}[Expected $p$-th power of $|f|$ on one bit]
\lean{OneBit.expect_abs_rpow_one_bit}
\leanok
\uses{BooleanAnalysis.BoolCube, BooleanAnalysis.BooleanFunc, BooleanAnalysis.expect, BooleanAnalysis.fourierCoeff, BooleanAnalysis.uniformWeight, OneBit.one_bit_val_false, OneBit.one_bit_val_true, OneBit.boolCube1_ne, OneBit.boolCube1_univ}
For a one-bit function $f$, writing $a = \widehat{f}(\emptyset)$ and $b = \widehat{f}(\{0\})$,
\[
  \E\big[|f|^p\big] = \frac{|a+b|^p + |a-b|^p}{2}.
\]
\end{lemma}

\begin{lemma}[$L^p$ norm monotonicity (power-mean inequality)]
\lean{OneBit.lp_norm_mono}
\leanok
\uses{BooleanAnalysis.BoolCube, BooleanAnalysis.BooleanFunc, BooleanAnalysis.expect, BooleanAnalysis.uniformWeight}
For $1 \le r \le s$ and any $f : \mathrm{BoolCube}\,n \to \bbr$,
\[
  \big(\E[|f|^r]\big)^{1/r} \le \big(\E[|f|^s]\big)^{1/s}.
\]
This is the power-mean inequality for the uniform probability measure on the cube.
\end{lemma}

\begin{theorem}[Two-point inequality on the unit interval]
\lean{OneBit.two_point_ineq_unit}
\leanok
For $1 \le p \le 2$ and $0 \le b \le 1$,
\[
  \big(1 + (p-1)b^2\big)^{p/2} \le \frac{(1+b)^p + (1-b)^p}{2}.
\]
\end{theorem}

\begin{lemma}[Two-point inequality: the $a=0$ case]
\lean{OneBit.two_point_ineq_a_zero}
\leanok
For $1 \le p \le 2$ one has $(p-1)^{p/2} \le 1$, which yields the two-point inequality in the
case $a = 0$.
\end{lemma}

\begin{lemma}[Noise operator does not increase the absolute-value $L^2$ contribution]
\lean{OneBit.noise_l2_abs_mono}
\leanok
For real $a, b$ and $0 \le \rho \le 1$, writing $u = a+b$ and $v = a-b$,
\[
  a^2 + \rho^2 b^2 \le \Big(\tfrac{|u| + |v|}{2}\Big)^2 + \rho^2 \Big(\tfrac{|u| - |v|}{2}\Big)^2 .
\]
\end{lemma}

\begin{theorem}[Two-point inequality (full version)]
\lean{OneBit.two_point_ineq}
\leanok
\uses{OneBit.two_point_ineq_unit}
For $1 \le p \le 2$, all $a, b \in \bbr$, and $\rho \ge 0$ with $\rho^2 \le p-1$,
\[
  \big(a^2 + \rho^2 b^2\big)^{1/2} \le \Big(\frac{|a+b|^p + |a-b|^p}{2}\Big)^{1/p}.
\]
\end{theorem}

\begin{theorem}[One-bit $(p,2)$-hypercontractivity]
\lean{OneBit.one_bit_p2_hypercontractivity}
\leanok
\uses{BooleanAnalysis.BooleanFunc, BooleanAnalysis.expect, BooleanAnalysis.fourierCoeff, BooleanAnalysis.noiseOp, OneBit.expect_abs_rpow_one_bit, OneBit.expect_noiseOp_sq_one_bit, OneBit.two_point_ineq}
For $f : \mathrm{BoolCube}\,1 \to \bbr$, $1 \le p \le 2$, and $\rho \ge 0$ with
$\rho^2 \le p-1$,
\[
  \big(\E[(T_\rho f)^2]\big)^{1/2} \le \big(\E[|f|^p]\big)^{1/p}.
\]
\end{theorem}

\begin{lemma}[Sign times value equals absolute value]
\lean{OneBit.sign_mul_self}
\leanok
For all real $x$, $\operatorname{sign}(x)\cdot x = |x|$.
\end{lemma}

\begin{lemma}[Absolute value of sign is one for nonzero input]
\lean{OneBit.abs_sign_eq_one}
\leanok
For $x \neq 0$, $|\operatorname{sign}(x)| = 1$.
\end{lemma}

\begin{lemma}[Expectation of a nonnegative function is nonnegative]
\lean{OneBit.expect_nonneg_of_nonneg}
\leanok
\uses{BooleanAnalysis.BooleanFunc, BooleanAnalysis.expect, BooleanAnalysis.uniformWeight}
If $f \ge 0$ pointwise, then $\E[f] \ge 0$.
\end{lemma}

\begin{lemma}[Expectation of a constant function]
\lean{OneBit.expect_const_eq}
\leanok
\uses{BooleanAnalysis.BoolCube, BooleanAnalysis.expect, BooleanAnalysis.uniformWeight}
The expectation over the cube of the constant function with value $c$ equals $c$.
\end{lemma}

\begin{lemma}[Cauchy--Schwarz for the Boolean inner product]
\lean{OneBit.cauchy_schwarz_bool}
\leanok
\uses{BooleanAnalysis.BoolCube, BooleanAnalysis.BooleanFunc, BooleanAnalysis.expect, BooleanAnalysis.innerProduct, BooleanAnalysis.uniformWeight}
For $f, g : \mathrm{BoolCube}\,n \to \bbr$,
\[
  \langle f, g\rangle \le \big(\E[f^2]\big)^{1/2}\,\big(\E[g^2]\big)^{1/2}.
\]
\end{lemma}

\begin{lemma}[H\"older sharpness]
\lean{OneBit.holder_sharpness}
\leanok
\uses{BooleanAnalysis.BooleanFunc, BooleanAnalysis.expect, BooleanAnalysis.innerProduct, OneBit.expect_nonneg_of_nonneg, OneBit.expect_const_eq}
For H\"older conjugate exponents $(p, q)$ and any function $u$, there exists a function $f$ with
$\norm{f}_p \le 1$ and $\norm{u}_q \le \langle f, u\rangle$.
\end{lemma}

\begin{theorem}[Noise operator duality]
\lean{OneBit.noise_operator_duality}
\leanok
\uses{Bonami.expect_sq_nonneg, BooleanAnalysis.BooleanFunc, BooleanAnalysis.expect, BooleanAnalysis.noiseOp, BooleanAnalysis.noiseOp_self_adjoint, OneBit.cauchy_schwarz_bool, OneBit.holder_sharpness}
If, for H\"older conjugate exponents $p$ and $p'$ with $1 \le p$, the noise operator with rate
$\sqrt{p-1}$ satisfies the $(p,2)$-hypercontractive bound for every one-bit function, then it also
satisfies the dual $(2,p')$-hypercontractive bound: for every $g$,
\[
  \big(\E[|T_{\sqrt{p-1}} g|^{p'}]\big)^{1/p'} \le \big(\E[g^2]\big)^{1/2}.
\]
\end{theorem}

\begin{theorem}[One-bit $(2,q)$-hypercontractivity]
\lean{OneBit.one_bit_2q_hypercontractivity}
\leanok
\uses{BooleanAnalysis.BooleanFunc, BooleanAnalysis.expect, BooleanAnalysis.noiseOp, OneBit.noise_operator_duality, OneBit.one_bit_p2_hypercontractivity}
For $g : \mathrm{BoolCube}\,1 \to \bbr$ and $q \ge 2$,
\[
  \norm{T_{1/\sqrt{q-1}}\, g}_q \le \norm{g}_2 .
\]
\end{theorem}
